name: Create Reusable Ansible Roles
description: Structure automation into reusable roles with tasks, handlers, templates, defaults, and metadata for sharing across projects.
trendingReason: Roles are the standard way to share and reuse automation, and Ansible Galaxy has 30,000+ community roles.

steps.title: Initialize a role skeleton
steps.description: Creates the standard role directory structure with tasks, handlers, defaults, vars, templates, files, and meta.

steps.title: Define default variables
steps.command:
cat > roles/nginx/defaults/main.yml << 'EOF'
---
nginx_port: 80
nginx_worker_processes: auto
nginx_server_name: localhost
EOF
steps.description: Defaults have the lowest precedence and can be overridden by users of the role.

steps.title: Write the main tasks
steps.command:
cat > roles/nginx/tasks/main.yml << 'EOF'
---
- name: Install nginx
  apt:
    name: nginx
    state: present
  notify: restart nginx

- name: Deploy nginx config
  template:
    src: nginx.conf.j2
    dest: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
  notify: restart nginx

- name: Ensure nginx is running
  service:
    name: nginx
    state: started
    enabled: true
EOF
steps.description: Tasks use handlers for service restarts and templates for dynamic configuration.

steps.title: Create a handler
steps.command:
cat > roles/nginx/handlers/main.yml << 'EOF'
---
- name: restart nginx
  service:
    name: nginx
    state: restarted
EOF
steps.description: Handlers run only when notified by a task that made a change, avoiding unnecessary restarts.

steps.title: Use the role in a playbook
steps.command:
cat > site.yml << 'EOF'
---
- name: Deploy web servers
  hosts: webservers
  become: true
  roles:
    - role: nginx
      nginx_port: 8080
      nginx_server_name: app.example.com
EOF
steps.description: Imports the role and overrides default variables at the play level.

fullScript:
# Create a reusable Ansible role
ansible-galaxy role init roles/nginx
cat > roles/nginx/defaults/main.yml << 'EOF'
---
nginx_port: 80
nginx_worker_processes: auto
nginx_server_name: localhost
EOF
cat > roles/nginx/tasks/main.yml << 'EOF'
---
- name: Install nginx
  apt:
    name: nginx
    state: present
  notify: restart nginx

- name: Deploy nginx config
  template:
    src: nginx.conf.j2
    dest: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
  notify: restart nginx

- name: Ensure nginx is running
  service:
    name: nginx
    state: started
    enabled: true
EOF
cat > roles/nginx/handlers/main.yml << 'EOF'
---
- name: restart nginx
  service:
    name: nginx
    state: restarted
EOF

faq.question: What is the difference between defaults and vars in a role?
faq.answer: defaults/main.yml has the lowest variable precedence and is meant to be overridden by users. vars/main.yml has higher precedence and is for internal role variables that should not be changed.

faq.question: How do I share a role on Ansible Galaxy?
faq.answer: Push the role to a public GitHub repository, then import it with 'ansible-galaxy role import <github_user> <repo_name>'. Add a meta/main.yml with galaxy_info for discoverability.

faq.question: Can a role depend on other roles?
faq.answer: Yes. List dependencies in meta/main.yml under 'dependencies:'. Ansible installs and runs dependent roles automatically before the current role.
